rdf:Description.dcterms:title: Geological Specimen Material Type
rdf:Description.skos:definition: The Geological Sample Material Type vocabulary is a SKOS-based hierarchical classification system for geological specimens. The scheme provides comprehensive coverage of the earth sciences domain by accommodating both natural and processed materials, terrestrial and extraterrestrial sources, and the practical needs of collection management across diverse geological institutions.
rdf:Description.dcterms:description: The Geological Sample Material Type vocabulary is a SKOS-based hierarchical classification system for geological specimens. The scheme provides comprehensive coverage of the earth sciences domain by accommodating both natural and processed materials, terrestrial and extraterrestrial sources, and the practical needs of collection management across diverse geological institutions.
rdf:Description.dcterms:provenance: Compiled by the TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group for community use and to support the extension release by providing standardized values for Material Entity Type, which is central to the Extension and Compound Specimen Model.
rdf:Description.skos:scopeNote: The Geologic Specimen Material Type vocabulary is intended to provide a top-level catagorization scheme for specimens that belong to earth sciences collections. Usage beyond the scope of earth sciences collections may require adjustments or broadening of specific definitions.
rdf:Description.http:: Intended to be used with the material category terms (e.g. dwc:materialEntityType) for specimens belonging to earth sciences and asteromaterials collections.
rdf:Description.skos:historyNote: Compiled by the TDWG Mineralogy Extension Task Group for community use and to support the extension release by providing standardized values for Material Entity Type, which is central to the Extension and Compound Specimen Model.
rdf:Description.skos:note: Concepts in the Geologic Specimen Material Type vocabulary are distinguished by compositional and physical characteristics. Top-level terms are based primarily on composition, while child terms represent physically distinct variants.
rdf:Description.sdo:citation: Norton B, Walcott R, Burri T, Puschnig A, Dombrowski A, Greber N, Hints O, Mansur A, Ralph J, Richard S, Tacker C. Geological Material Sample Type Vocabulary (pending).
rdf:Description.prov:wasDerivedFrom: Geoscience Ontology, Rock TypesGeoscience Ontology, Rock Types
Rubin AE, Grossman JN (2014) Meteorite and meteoroid: a new comprehensive definition. Meteorit Planet Sci 45:114–122
McGraw-Hill. (2003). McGraw-Hill Dictionary of Geology & Mineralogy (2nd ed.). McGraw Hill
